title: ocimum obovatum subsp. cordatum
intro: Ocimum obovatum subsp. cordatum, also known as wild basil, is a subspecies of the Ocimum obovatum plant, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Ocimum obovatum subsp. cordatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as poultices or creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
